Title: Suppressing wheat sucrose phosphate synthase 1-B protects wheat against stripe rust

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SPS family; UDP-binding domain (PF00534); glycosyltransferase 4-like domain (PF13579); C-terminal sucrose-phosphatase-like domain (PF05116)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes conversion of UDP-glucose and fructose-6-phosphate into sucrose-6-phosphate
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control step/pivotal molecular switch in pathogen-hijacked sucrose metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major transport sugar and energy source; supports Puccinia striiformis f. sp. tritici growth during infection
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Group I SPS1 genes show relatively high expression in wheat leaves; SPS1-B is induced during Pst infection, especially at 16°C
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by Pst infection in leaves at 16°C; lower expression at 22°C
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pst infection; temperature (16°C permissive for stronger induction, 22°C suppressive)
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Ubi promoter (overexpression in wheat); 35S promoter (subcellular localization in tobacco)
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: BSR-Seq/RNA-seq, NCBI GEO: PRJNA877083; public RNA-seq reanalysis: PRJNA243835; PRJEB12497
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes sucrose accumulation and wheat susceptibility to stripe rust; loss-of-function reduces sucrose and restricts Pst growth without compromising yield
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SPS1-B was identified by BSR-Seq as the causal gene underlying the spy1 susceptibility phenotype and encodes a cytoplasmic sucrose-phosphate synthase. 2. Loss-of-function sps1-B alleles reduced SPS activity and sucrose accumulation after Pst infection, restricting fungal growth and pustule formation. 3. Overexpression of SPS1-B increased SPS1 protein abundance and markedly enhanced Pst pustule density, demonstrating sufficiency for susceptibility. 4. SPS1-B is transcriptionally induced by Pst at 16°C and suppressed at high temperature-associated resistance conditions. 5. SPS1-B is negatively regulated by wAP2 and post-translationally suppressed by WKS1.1 phosphorylation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: EMS mutant mapping and co-segregation; independent loss-of-function mutants; overexpression in wheat; enzyme assay; metabolite quantification; subcellular localization; qRT-PCR; Western blot; pathogen biomass/pustule quantification
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: sps1-B single mutants showed reduced starch content and increased grain protein content, but no significant grain weight or yield penalty under tested conditions; combined sps1-A/B mutations reduced kernel weight
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising susceptibility-gene editing target for enhancing stripe rust resistance with minimal yield penalty; suitable for pyramiding with wAP2 and Yr36/WKS1
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: SPS1-B, rather than SPS1-A, is the primary Pst-responsive SPS1 homeolog linked to susceptibility; overexpression also correlated with increased expression of sugar transporters SWEET2b2, SWEET16a, SUT3, TaSTP6, and TaSTP13

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes formation of sucrose-6-phosphate from UDP-glucose and fructose-6-phosphate
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established for stripe rust susceptibility
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major transport sugar and energy source
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Important contributor to basal SPS protein/activity in leaves under non-infected conditions
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expression induced with total SPS1 at 16°C after Pst infection, but not the primary transcriptional target at 22°C
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes strongly to basal SPS activity in leaves, but its loss alone did not substantially mitigate Pst growth
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SPS1-A is a homeolog of SPS1-B and contributes substantially to total SPS protein abundance and basal SPS activity in wheat leaves. 2. The sps1-A single mutant did not significantly reduce stripe rust growth, unlike sps1-B mutants. 3. Double mutation with sps1-B nearly abolished detectable SPS1 protein and further reduced total SPS activity, but caused reduced kernel weight.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: EMS mutant analysis; double-mutant analysis; enzyme activity assay; Western blot; disease phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: sps1-A single mutant and sps1-A/B1 double mutant showed lower 1000-kernel weight; mutants also had lower starch and higher protein content
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Less favorable standalone editing target than SPS1-B for stripe rust resistance because of weaker resistance effect and stronger yield penalty
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The SPS1-A promoter showed strong auto-activation in Y1H, preventing similar upstream TF screening; SPS1-A appears dominant without infection, whereas SPS1-B is preferentially hijacked by Pst

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major transport sugar and energy source; reduced sucrose availability restricts Pst growth
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Sucrose biosynthesis via transcriptional repression of SPS1-B
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent negative regulator that suppresses sucrose accumulation and lowers stripe rust susceptibility
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Pst infection at 22°C strongly induces wAP2-A
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in response to Pst at 22°C
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Ubi promoter (overexpression in wheat); 35S promoter (subcellular localization/heterologous assays)
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Suppresses SPS1-B expression and protein accumulation, reduces sucrose content, and enhances wheat resistance to stripe rust
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. wAP2-A was identified by Y1H screening as a direct binder of the SPS1-B promoter. 2. EMSA and dual-luciferase assays showed that wAP2-A directly represses SPS1-B transcription. 3. wAP2-A localizes to the nucleus and is strongly induced by Pst at 22°C. 4. Overexpression of wAP2-A in wheat reduced SPS1 protein, lowered sucrose content, and decreased Pst pustule density by more than 50%.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H screening; EMSA; dual-luciferase assay; subcellular localization; overexpression in wheat; Western blot; sucrose measurement; disease phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No clear growth or yield penalty reported in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful regulatory target for transgenic or allele-based suppression of SPS1-B; can be pyramided with sps1-B and Yr36/WKS1
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: wAP2-B also bound and repressed the SPS1-B promoter in transient assays, but functional transgenic validation focused on wAP2-A

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Triticum turgidum var. durum; Triticum aestivum
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Kinase-START protein; kinase domain plus START domain
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major transport sugar and energy source; lowering sucrose restricts pathogen growth
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Post-translational suppression of sucrose biosynthesis enzyme activity and abundance
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Suppresses SPS1-B protein abundance and activity via phosphorylation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Protein binding, phosphorylation, and promotion of protein degradation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: High-temperature-associated resistance regulator that lowers sucrose content by inactivating and destabilizing SPS1-B
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Pst infection at 22°C; high-temperature adult-plant resistance context
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms protein complex with SPS1-B through the interlinker-START region
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: WKS1 protein is induced by Pst at 22°C but not at 16°C
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Protein accumulation increases upon Pst infection at 22°C
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter (transgenic lines); Ubi promoter for TAP-WKS1.1 in co-IP experiments
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Co-IP mass spectrometry identified SPS1 peptides in TAP-WKS1.1 products
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduces sucrose content and enhances resistance to stripe rust by binding, phosphorylating, reducing enzymatic activity of, and promoting degradation of SPS1-B
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. WKS1.1 transgenic wheat showed sharply reduced sucrose content and lower SPS activity/protein levels. 2. Co-IP/MS, Y2H, pull-down, and split-LUC demonstrated physical interaction between WKS1.1 and SPS1-B. 3. In vitro kinase assays showed WKS1.1 phosphorylates SPS1-B. 4. Phosphorylation reduced SPS1-B enzymatic activity and promoted its degradation. 5. Combining WKS1 with sps1-B mutations further enhanced resistance, reaching immunity-like phenotype at 22°C in seedlings.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic analysis; co-IP/MS; Y2H; pull-down; split-LUC; in vitro phosphorylation assays; SPS enzyme assay; degradation assay; sucrose quantification; disease phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No direct nutrient-yield penalty reported here; resistance is temperature-associated and partial when alone
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for gene pyramiding with sps1-B alleles to suppress sucrose supply to Pst and improve stripe rust resistance
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Originally characterized as high-temperature adult-plant resistance gene Yr36; here also showed seedling-stage partial resistance at 22°C in Kronos background
